prompt: How does very low body fat affect strength and hypertrophy progress, and how can someone know if they’re too lean given poor body-fat measurement tools?
expected_output: They said very low body fat generally makes both performance and hypertrophy progress much harder due to reduced energy availability and impaired recovery. Eric described practical signs during contest-level leanness: worse training quality, slower recovery, colder body temperature, higher hunger, and broad 'system slowdown' effects. He also noted that in their p-ratio review, substantial lean-mass gains were uncommon below roughly 8% body fat in men. For monitoring, they advised against over-relying on imperfect body-fat devices and instead emphasized symptom-based and performance-based feedback. In other words, individual functional signs of low energy availability are more actionable than chasing one exact body-fat number.
topic: very low body fat and training outcomes
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on very low body fat and training outcomes
